breaking_news.title: State-Sponsored Actors Deploy DarkSword iOS Exploit Chain for Surveillance
breaking_news.summary:
Google Threat Intelligence Group identified a new iOS exploit chain named DarkSword that leverages six zero-day vulnerabilities to fully compromise devices. This sophisticated tool is being utilized by multiple commercial surveillance vendors and suspected state-sponsored actors to deploy malware payloads like GHOSTBLADE.

The campaign targets users in regions including Saudi Arabia, Turkey, Malaysia, and Ukraine, posing a severe risk to mobile security and privacy. Apple devices running iOS versions 18.4 through 18.7 are currently vulnerable to this full-chain exploit that enables data theft without user interaction.

breaking_news.title: Interlock Ransomware Exploits Cisco Firewall Zero-Day for Root Access
breaking_news.summary:
The Interlock ransomware gang has been actively exploiting a maximum-severity remote code execution vulnerability in Cisco Secure Firewall Management Center software since late January. Amazon Threat Intelligence confirmed that CVE-2026-20131 was abused as a zero-day for over a month before Cisco issued a patch.

This critical flaw allows unauthenticated attackers to gain root access to network infrastructure, leading to widespread ransomware deployments across enterprise environments. Organizations relying on Cisco firewalls face immediate risk of network compromise and operational disruption due to the severity of the vulnerability.

breaking_news.title: GlassWorm Supply Chain Attack Compromises 400+ GitHub and npm Repositories
breaking_news.summary:
A coordinated supply-chain attack dubbed GlassWorm has compromised over 400 code repositories on GitHub, npm, and VSCode extension registries. Threat actors used stolen GitHub tokens to force-push malicious code into popular Python projects and developer tools.

This campaign targets software developers by injecting malware into trusted dependencies, potentially affecting millions of downstream users who install the compromised packages. The attack highlights significant vulnerabilities in the software supply chain and the risks associated with extension dependency abuse.

breaking_news.title: Stryker Medtech Giant Hit by Handala Hacktivists in Device Wipe Attack
breaking_news.summary:
Medical technology giant Stryker confirmed a cyberattack that remotely wiped tens of thousands of employee devices without using traditional malware. The pro-Palestinian hacktivist group Handala claimed responsibility for the disruption, which forced the company to shut down offices across 79 countries.

While medical devices remain safe, electronic ordering systems are offline, impacting hospital operations and patient care globally. This incident underscores the growing threat of hacktivist groups targeting critical healthcare infrastructure during geopolitical conflicts.

breaking_news.title: Critical Unpatched Telnetd Flaw Enables Remote Root Code Execution
breaking_news.summary:
Cybersecurity researchers disclosed a critical unpatched vulnerability in GNU InetUtils telnetd that allows unauthenticated remote attackers to execute code with elevated privileges. Tracked as CVE-2026-32746, the flaw carries a CVSS score of 9.8 and stems from an out-of-bounds write in the LINEMODE handler.

This vulnerability affects all versions up to 2.7 and could enable attackers to gain full root access to vulnerable systems via the Telnet protocol. Administrators are urged to update as soon as a patch becomes available to prevent potential remote compromise of legacy network services.

breaking_news.title: OFAC Sanctions North Korean IT Worker Network Funding WMD Programs
breaking_news.summary:
The U.S. Department of the Treasury sanctioned six individuals and two entities involved in a North Korean IT worker scheme designed to defraud businesses. This network funnels illicit revenue to the regime to fund weapons of mass destruction programs through fake remote job offers.

The sanctions target a sophisticated operation that infiltrates Western companies to steal sensitive information and generate over $500 million annually for the DPRK. This action highlights the intersection of cybercrime and state-sponsored proliferation efforts targeting the global financial sector.

breaking_news.title: UK Companies House Flaw Exposed Millions of Firms and Director Details
breaking_news.summary:
The UK government agency Companies House confirmed a security flaw in its WebFiling service exposed details of millions of firms and company directors. The vulnerability allowed unauthorized users to view confidential paperwork and alter company records before the service was taken offline.

This breach compromises the integrity of the UK corporate registry and exposes sensitive business data to potential misuse by malicious actors. The incident has forced a temporary shutdown of the filing platform to implement necessary security fixes and restore trust.

breaking_news.title: Nine Critical AppArmor Flaws Risk 12 Million Enterprise Linux Systems
breaking_news.summary:
Qualys researchers disclosed nine critical vulnerabilities in the Linux AppArmor security module that could allow unprivileged users to gain root access. Dubbed CrackArmor, these flaws have existed since 2017 and affect over 12 million enterprise systems running Ubuntu, Debian, and SUSE.

Attackers can exploit these bugs to break out of container isolation and crash systems without requiring administrative credentials. The widespread deployment of AppArmor across cloud platforms and IoT devices makes this a significant risk for enterprise Linux environments.
